all_text: Final   qualifying   work (performance of the vocal concert program) direction 53.03.03 "Vocal art" Semin   Evgeniy   Yuryevich Program: 1. K. V. Gluck - Paris 'aria" O del  mio  dolce ardor " from the opera "Paris and Elena". 2. E. Grieg, poems by T. Banville " Ich  liebe  dich (I love you») 3. G. Donizetti-Nemorino's romance "Una furtiva  lagrima " from the opera "  L'elisir   d'amore ". 4. N. A. Rimsky-Korsakov- Sadko's  song "  Ой ,  ты   темная   дубравушка " from the opera " Sadko ". 5. G. G.  Hanedanyan , poems by A. A.  Fet  - " Тихая ,  звездная   ночь ". 6. N. A. Rimsky-Korsakov, words by A. A. Tolstoy – « Не   ветер   вея   с   высоты ». Supervisor of the FQW: Honored Artist of Ukraine, People's Artist of Uzbekistan and the Republic of North Ossetia - Alania, Professor of the St. Petersburg University  Hrair   Hrayrovich   Khanedanyan . Annotation . The final qualifying work is a vocal concert program composed of classical and contemporary vocal music. The program includes the aria "O del mio dolce ardor" from the opera "Paris and Elena", which represents the vocal technique of Baroque (Italy, mid-18th century). The foreign opera school of the mid-19th century is represented by the aria "Una furtiva lagrima" from Gaetano Donizetti's famous opera "" L'elisir d'amore ". Edvard Grieg's "Ich liebe dich" ("I love you") is an example of German chamber vocal technique and the romance "  Не   ветер   вея   с   высоты  " by Nikolai Rimsky- Korsakov, based on the poems of Alexey Konstantinovich Tolstoy, reflects Russian chamber vocal technique. The program also includes Sadko's song ""  Ой , ты   темная   дубравушка  " from the opera "Sadko", as a sample of a prominent Russian opera vocal school. The program concludes with the romance "  Тихая , звездная   ночь  " by the contemporary composer Hrair Hrairovich Hanedanyan, whose distinctive features are subtle, refined melodic nuances, expressive poetry, and oriental flavor in the piano and voice parts.
